subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/EFF, World Bank, IDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, ACLED) + Ecuadorian official (INEC, the finance ministry) + Ecuadorian/Spanish-language press (El Universo, Primicias, GK).

Ecuador is fully DOLLARIZED (it uses the US dollar) - it has no sovereign monetary policy, which anchors stability and low inflation but removes the devaluation lever; assets carry no local-currency devaluation risk.

Standout drags: a catastrophic security collapse (a declared internal armed conflict against cartels), weak/narco-infiltrated rule of law, and an energy crisis; genuine offsets are dollar-stability, low inflation, and an open economy. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone; Spanish primary sources under-read.

economic.notes: Dollar-anchored stability and low inflation, against high financing needs, oil dependence, and an energy crisis.

fiscal_state.notes: High financing needs and oil-revenue dependence under an IMF program, with a deficit overshooting target but reforms attempted. (IMF CR 25/199)

monetary_independence.notes: Fully dollarized - no devalue/print lever and imported US policy (a loss of autonomy), but a credible discipline/stability anchor that removes the classic FX-crisis channel. (IMF; BCE)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: It uses the US dollar itself, so cash and local assets carry no local-currency devaluation risk - a real safety feature. (definitional; BCE)

inflation.notes: Low (~2%), an imported dollar-stability strength. (INEC; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Reasonably sound and devaluation-insulated by dollarisation, but with no lender of last resort and thin reserves. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil plus diversifying shrimp/cocoa/banana exports, against structural oil-output decline and a 2024 drought-driven electricity crisis. (BCE; OilPrice)

institutional.notes: Usable statistics, against a weak, narco-infiltrated rule of law and a threatened press.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak and deteriorating, with narco-infiltration of the justice system and corruption. (WJP; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Official statistics are usable and published, with some politicisation risk. (INEC; BCE)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and unstable, with thin state presence in contested coastal provinces. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Legally free but with journalists killed and exiled under cartel and state threats. (RSF 2025/2026)

political_social.notes: Lawful migration handling, against political volatility, militarised-security strain, and deep cleavages.

political_stability.notes: Volatile - the government (re-elected in 2025 with an assembly majority, a partial stabiliser) governs under serial states of emergency and a declared 'internal armed conflict', against a 2023 congressional-dissolution precedent. (CEPR)

civil_liberties.notes: Militarised security erodes some rights, with a 2025 fuel-protest crackdown. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful routes that absorbed large Venezuelan migration, with support eroding. (IOM)

social_cohesion.notes: High inequality, recurrent indigenous-movement strikes, and coast-sierra divides. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: Still relatively young, with falling fertility and emigration pressure. (INEC)

geopolitical.notes: An open, US-aligned posture, dominated by an internal-security war.

alliance_reliability.notes: Pragmatic and pro-US on security cooperation. (press)

conflict_involvement.notes: A declared internal armed conflict against cartels - an active internal-security war. (government decree; ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open, dollarized, unsanctioned, with no capital controls. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Seismic and climate exposure, a weak health system, an energy crisis, and a catastrophic crime collapse.

climate.notes: Andean glacier/water loss plus strong-ENSO flooding plus Galapagos exposure, low readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: A weak, uneven public system with drug/equipment shortages and long waits. (Frontiers 2025)

infrastructure.notes: The 2024 nationwide electricity crisis was a major structural drag despite 2025 easing; roads are mixed. (Al Jazeera)

crime_safety.notes: A catastrophic collapse - a homicide rate that exploded to among the region's highest amid cartel and prison wars, having become a cocaine-trafficking hub. (National Police; InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against severe seismic/flood exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A fully dollarized economy whose genuine strengths - dollar-anchored stability, low inflation, no local-currency devaluation risk, and an open capital account - are dominated by a catastrophic security collapse: a declared internal armed conflict against cartels, a homicide rate that exploded to among the region's highest, and a justice system infiltrated by organized crime. High fiscal financing needs under an IMF program, structural oil decline, a 2024 drought-driven electricity crisis, political volatility, and seismic/climate exposure round out a negative profile. Dollarization removes the FX-crisis lever but also any monetary flexibility.
